reasoning: Elon Musk departed the Trump administration on May 28, 2025, precisely because he was reaching the 130-day limit for Special Government Employees (SGEs) within a 365-day period. By law, exceeding this cap would require him to divest his massive private holdings (Tesla, SpaceX, X, etc.) and comply with strict conflict-of-interest rules, which he will not do. Because the rule restricts him to 130 days in *any* 365-day period, he legally cannot serve as an SGE again until early 2026. While Musk and Trump recently reconciled after a public summer feud, Musk has expressed disillusionment with the government, calling it "unfixable." Given the absolute legal barrier to him taking a role in 2025 without divesting, the lack of remaining time in the year, and his own stated lack of interest in returning to his "side quest" in Washington, the chance of an official nomination or appointment by December 31, 2025, is extremely low.
